claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit! Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about your child's age:**

How old is the child you're claiming Child Benefit for?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know which age category applies to your child.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I'm looking to claim Child Benefit for my son Alex. He's 18 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you're looking to claim Child Benefit for your son Alex, who is 18 years old.

Since Alex is between 16 and 19, I need to check whether he meets certain education or training requirements. Let me navigate to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
For young people aged 16-19 like Alex, Child Benefit eligibility depends on their education or training status.

**Which of the following best describes Alex's current situation?**

1. **In education or training** - Alex is currently enrolled in a qualifying course or approved training programme

2. **Recently left education** - Alex has left education or training within the last few months and may be eligible for an extension period

3. **Neither** - Alex is not in education/training and hasn't recently left

Please let me know which applies to Alex.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex isn't in education or training at the moment. He finished school a while back and he's not doing any courses right now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Since Alex finished school "a while back" and isn't currently in education or training, I need to check if he might still qualify for an extension period.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see that there's an extension period of up to **20 weeks** after leaving education, but this only applies to those aged 18 or under.

Since Alex is 18, he could potentially qualify for this extension - but only if he left education recently (within the last 20 weeks). You mentioned he finished school "a while back."

**Can I ask: roughly when did Alex finish his education?**

- Was it **within the last 20 weeks** (about 5 months)?
- Or was it **longer than 20 weeks ago**?

This is important because if it's been more than 20 weeks since he left education, unfortunately he wouldn't qualify for the extension period.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, it's been longer than that. He left school quite a while ago now, definitely more than five months.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Since Alex:

- Is **18 years old** (between 16-19)
- Is **not currently in education or training**
- Left education **more than 20 weeks ago** (so doesn't qualify for the extension period)

I need to navigate to the outcome for this situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I now have all the information I need to provide you with an eligibility outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## Child Benefit Eligibility Result

**Result: NOT ELIGIBLE** ❌

I'm sorry to say that based on the information you've provided, you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

### Why is Alex not eligible?

For children aged 16-19, Child Benefit can only be claimed if they are:
- In **approved full-time education** (at least 12 hours per week of supervised study), OR
- In **approved training**, OR
- Within **20 weeks of leaving education** and registered with a careers service

Since Alex is 18, not currently in education or training, and left school more than 20 weeks ago, he doesn't meet any of these qualifying criteria.

---

### What are your options?

1. **If Alex returns to education**: If Alex decides to enrol in an approved course (such as A-levels, T-levels, BTECs, or NVQs at Level 1-3) with at least 12 hours of supervised study per week, you may become eligible again. Let HMRC know if this happens.

2. **Other support**: Even though Child Benefit isn't available, Alex may be eligible for other support such as:
   - Universal Credit (if he's looking for work)
   - Education grants or bursaries if he returns to study

---

I know this isn't the news you were hoping for. Is there anything else I can help you with, or do you have any questions about this decision?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old. For children aged 16-19, Child Benefit is only payable if they are in approved full-time education or training (at least 12 hours per week of supervised study at a qualifying level). Alex is not currently in education or training. Additionally, there is an extension period of up to 20 weeks after leaving education for those aged 18 or under who register with a careers service, but Alex left education more than 20 weeks ago, so this extension does not apply.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex because Alex is 18 years old, not in approved education or training, and the 20-week extension period after leaving education has already passed.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.description: For 16-19 year olds, check if in education/training or eligible for extension
response.routing_logic: If in education/training, validate type and level. If just left education (within 20 weeks), check extension eligibility.

age_limits.birthday_rule: Benefit stops on child's 20th birthday, not end of academic year

training_exclusions: Training with employer agreement for future job

payment_rates.note: Current rates not specified in source documents - check gov.uk for latest rates

high_income_charge.note: High Income Child Benefit Charge applies if you or partner earns over £60,000. This is a payment adjustment, not an eligibility criterion.

response.reason: No qualifying child under 16, or under 20 in approved education/training
response.guidance: Child must be under 16, or aged 16-19 and in approved education or training. If child just turned 20, benefit stops. If 16-17 year old leaves education, benefit can continue for 20 weeks if they register with careers service or armed services.
